name: cpig-kin-11-strandbeest-leg
description: kin-11 Strandbeest-style walking leg — planar closed-loop 4-bar inspired by Theo Jansen's linkage. Crank + coupler + rocker + ground form one closed loop; a foot vertex on the coupler traces a coupler curve as the crank rotates under 200 N·mm torque. Forward-dynamics integrates 2s; 5 Pose at Step taps (steps 0/500/1000/1500/2000) flatten into Pose Bound Bodies, rendering five overlapping coupler-triangle outlines that capture the leg's swing motion in one frame. Topology — NOT the full Jansen 13-bar linkage (which has 2 closed loops + 2 rigid triangle bodies); this simplification is the minimum that stress-tests the planar closed-loop solver. Phase 4.6.F.3 closed-loop solver demo.

setup.vlmDescription: A Strandbeest-style walking-leg linkage shown as a stop-motion strip of five overlapping triangular shapes (the coupler-triangle B-C-F, rendered as a thin closed pipe outline) drawn at five different crank angles spanning a 2-second forward-dynamics integration under 200 N·mm constant crank torque. The triangles rotate and swing about the design-pose pivot points: O (crank hub at world origin), P (rocker hub at (-25,25,0)), and the moving pivots B (crank-coupler join) and C (coupler-rocker join). The free vertex F (the foot) sweeps a closed coupler curve — a smooth oval-to-kidney-bean shape under the linkage. The kinematic skeleton (design-pose 4-bar frame triads + joint circles) is drawn at 50% alpha behind the moving triangles via Preview Diagram in Display Mode 2.
